figures.caption: Fig. 1 The left column is a snapshot of the true solution (t = 0 for the left plot and t = 1 for the right plot) and the right column is the neural net prediction. The rows represent the three components of the solution, u, v, and p. The domain is (x, y) ∈ [0, 2]2, with the viscosity set to 10−2 and the density set to 1.

Fig. 2 A scatter plot showing the relation between the generalization error upperbound given in Theorem 1 and the generalization error calculated after training of the tanh3 activated PINNs. Each point is labeled by the Nr/N0 ratio at which the net was trained. On the left, the viscosity is set to ν = 0.001 and on the right ν = 0.01.
